zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel Elizabeth Stewart於1437年1月出生在蘇格蘭阿吉爾格勒諾爾時，她的父親John Stewart 2nd Lord of Lorne40歲，母親Lady Agnes MacDonald38歲。她於1453年4月9日與Sir Colin Campbell, 1st Earl of Argyll在蘇格蘭阿吉爾格勒諾爾 安德 音尼沙艾爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和8個女兒的父母。她去世於1510年10月26日在蘇格蘭可拉可曼南史爾多拉亞享年73歲，埋葬在英國蘇格蘭阿吉爾 安德 布特吉爾蒙吉爾蒙 帕里什 邱奇 安德 其特利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Isabel Elizabeth Stewart于1437年1月出生在苏格兰阿吉爾格勒諾爾时，她的父亲John Stewart 2nd Lord of Lorne40岁，母亲Lady Agnes MacDonald38岁。她于1453年4月9日与Sir Colin Campbell, 1st Earl of Argyll在苏格兰阿吉爾格勒諾爾 安德 音尼沙艾爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和8个女儿的父母。她去世于1510年10月26日在苏格兰可拉可曼南史爾多拉亞享年73岁，埋葬在英国苏格兰阿吉爾 安德 布特吉爾蒙吉爾蒙 帕里什 邱奇 安德 其特利。

nameMeanings.name: Stewart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (Lanarkshire) and English: originally an occupational name for an administrative official of an estate, from Middle English stiward, Old English stigweard, stīweard, a compound of stig ‘house(hold)’ + weard ‘guardian’. In the Anglo-Saxon period this title was used of an officer controlling the domestic affairs of a household, especially of the royal household; after the Norman Conquest it was also used more widely as the native equivalent of Seneschal, for the steward of a manor or manager of an estate. In Scotland the term was also used of a magistrate originally appointed by the king to administer crown lands, forming a stewartry.

History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland, the royal family of Scotland from the 14th century, and of England from 1603, when James VI of Scotland acceded to the English throne as James I. There were many minor branches of the family left in Britain after the flight of James II in 1688, but not every bearer of the surname can claim relationship with the royal house, even in Scotland. Every great house in medieval England and Scotland had its steward, and in many cases the office gave rise to a hereditary surname. The fall of the house of Stuart in Britain, conversely, led to the establishment of several highly placed branches bearing this surname in continental Europe, which are in most cases related to the old Scottish royal family.

variants.name: Stuart
meanings: Scottish (Aberdeenshire and Banffshire): variant of   Stewart  . This French form of the name, there being no W in the French language, was adopted by Mary, Queen of Scots, on her return to Scotland in 1561 and has since come to be more popular among royalists than the earlier spellings.
History: Stuart or Stewart is the surname of one of the great families of Scotland (see note at   Stewart  ).

variants.name: Stewardson
meanings: English (Lancashire and Westmorland): patronymic from Middle English stiward ‘steward’ (see Stewart ) + -son ‘son of the steward’.

variants.name: Steward
meanings: English (London and East Anglia): variant of   Stewart  .

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: form of   Isabel  , which has been popular in England since the 18th century and has recently become the preferred form of the name.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel Elizabeth Stewart was born in January 1437, in Glenorchy, Argyll, Scotland, her father, John Stewart 2nd Lord of Lorne, was 40 and her mother, Lady Agnes MacDonald, was 38. She married Sir Colin Campbell, 1st Earl of Argyll on 9 April 1453, in Glenorchy and Inishail, Argyll, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 8 daughters. She died on 26 October 1510, in Dollar, Clackmannanshire, Scotland, at the age of 73, and was buried in Kilmun Parish Church And Cemetery, Kilmun, Argyll and Bute, Scotland, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Elizabeth Stewart è nata il nel gennaio 1437 nel Glenorchy, Argyll, Scotland, sua padre, John Stewart 2nd Lord of Lorne, aveva 40 anni e sua madre, Lady Agnes MacDonald, aveva 38 anni. Lei sposato Sir Colin Campbell, 1st Earl of Argyll il 9 aprile 1453, nel Glenorchy and Inishail, Argyll, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 8 figlie. Morì il 26 ottobre 1510, nel Dollar, Clackmannanshire, Scotland, all'età di 73 anni, e fu sepolta a Kilmun Parish Church And Cemetery, Kilmun, Argyll and Bute, Scozia, Regno Unito.

ja.Argyll, Scotland: スコットランドアルグイル
ja.lifeSummary: Isabel Elizabeth Stewartが1437年1月にスコットランドアルグイルグレノーチイで生まれた時，父John Stewart 2nd Lord of Lorneは40歳，母Lady Agnes MacDonaldは38歳であった。1453年4月9日にスコットランドアルグイルグレノーチイ アンド イニシャイルでSir Colin Campbell, 1st Earl of Argyllと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と8人の娘の親であった。彼女は1510年10月26日にスコットランドクラクマナンシュアイアドラーで73歳で死去し，イギリススコットランドアルグイル アンド ブトキルムンキルムン パーイッシュ チャーチ アンド セメテリーにて埋葬された。

ko.Inveraray Castle, Scotland: 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 앤드 부트 인베라라이
ko.Kilmun Parish Church and Cemetery, Kilmu: 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 앤드 부트 킬먼 킬먼 파리시 처르치 앤드 세멧리
ko.Dunstaffnage Castle, Oban, Scotland: 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 앤드 부트 오반
ko.lifeSummary: Isabel Elizabeth Stewart(이)가 1437년 1월 스컷랜드 아그이 글렌르c히에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Stewart 2nd Lord of Lorne(은)는 40세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Agnes MacDonald(은)는 38세였다. 그녀는 1453년 4월 9일 스컷랜드 아그이 글렌르c히 앤드 이니샤일에서 Sir Colin Campbell, 1st Earl of Argyll(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1510년 10월 26일 스컷랜드 클랙먼아실르 돌라에서 73세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 스코틀랜드 아그이 앤드 부트 킬먼 킬먼 파리시 처르치 앤드 세멧리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel Elizabeth Stewart nasceu em janeiro de 1437, em Glenorchy, Argyll, Scotland, seu pai, John Stewart 2nd Lord of Lorne, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Lady Agnes MacDonald, tinha 38 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Colin Campbell, 1st Earl of Argyll em 9 de abril de 1453, em Glenorchy and Inishail, Argyll, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 8 filhas. Ela faleceu em 26 de outubro de 1510, em Dollar, Clackmannanshire, Scotland, com 73 anos, e foi sepultada em Kilmun Parish Church And Cemetery, Kilmun, Argyll and Bute, Escócia, United Kingdom.

ru.Inveraray Castle, Scotland: инвирареи, аргилл анд бут, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.Kilmun Parish Church and Cemetery, Kilmu: килмун пэриш чурч анд кеметири, килмун, аргилл анд бут, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.Dunstaffnage Castle, Oban, Scotland: обань, аргилл анд бут, Шотландия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel Elizabeth Stewart родилась январь 1437 г. в местности гленорчи, аргилл, Шотландия, её отцу по имени John Stewart 2nd Lord of Lorne было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Agnes MacDonald, 38. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Colin Campbell, 1st Earl of Argyll 9 апреля 1453 г. в местности гленорчи анд аинишеил, аргилл, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 8 дочерей. Она умерла 26 октября 1510 г. в местности доллар, клаккманнаньшир, Шотландия в возрасте 73 лет и была похоронена в местности килмун пэриш чурч анд кеметири, килмун, аргилл анд бут, Шотландия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel Elizabeth Stewart nació en enero de 1437, en Glenorchy, Argyll, Scotland, su papá, John Stewart 2nd Lord of Lorne, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Lady Agnes MacDonald, tenía 38 años. Se casó con Sir Colin Campbell, 1st Earl of Argyll el 9 de abril de 1453, en Glenorchy and Inishail, Argyll, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 8 hijas. Murió el 26 de octubre de 1510, en Dollar, Clackmannanshire, Scotland, a la edad de 73 años, y fue sepultada en Kilmun Parish Church And Cemetery, Kilmun, Argyll and Bute, Escocia, Reino Unido.
